title: HeadObject, GetObject, Ranges, and SdkStream Cleanup · Scenario Checkpoint
summary: A production board compares near-miss TypeScript and AWS decisions for headobject, getobject, ranges, and sdkstream cleanup and accepts only runtime validation, bounded ownership, causal changed-case evidence, and an explicit controlled AWS transfer gate. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for bounded TypeScript download flow. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, choose HEAD, GET, conditional GET, or a metadata database lookup without doubling requests or trusting stale authority.
In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, check response and metadata, own and close the body, propagate cancellation, handle partial reads, and distinguish transport from caller writes.
In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, map application validators and preconditions to S3 requests and preserve 304, 412, not-found, and version-specific meaning.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Head Vs Get · changed case · Quiz Head Vs Get
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz TypeScript file-delivery team handles case 5861 during a Node stream backpressure incident. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to validate a versioned conditional range and prove SdkStream cleanup; bound bytes, time, attempts, promises, streams, concurrency, cache, and spend; then double concurrent Upload instances and preserve aggregate memory and worker budgets. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. Node and AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, PostgreSQL, credentials, host state, handle and stream checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to validate a versioned conditional range and prove SdkStream cleanup; do not treat TypeScript types, a resolved promise, CORS, ETag, or an emulator as stronger proof than it is; then abort during a stream or multipart promise and prove every task and part reaches a terminal state. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Download Error Boundary
steps.instruction:
Write the quiz evidence note for Download Error Boundary: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A quiz TypeScript file-delivery team handles case 5954 during a AWS SDK v3 dependency canary. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to validate a versioned conditional range and prove SdkStream cleanup; separate deterministic browser evidence from controlled Node and AWS transfer gates; then change object version, signed header, or cache dimension and reject stale authority. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. Node and AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, PostgreSQL, credentials, host state, handle and stream checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, classify missing key, missing version, delete marker, denied access, archive restore state, throttling, cancellation, and partial downstream response.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, classify missing key, missing version, delete marker, denied access, archive restore state, throttling, cancellation, and partial downstream response.
